tldr: Elizabeth, about 15 of your 28 live ads are wearing out because they use the exact same body text word-for-word. Meta sees these near-copies as basically one ad, so it quietly picks one and stops running the rest.

benchmark.context: The typical jewelry brand puts out fresh ads every three weeks. The best brands do it every two. You're posting new ads often, but so many say the exact same thing that Meta counts them as one.

ads.headline: Handcrafted Crystal Jewelry Made in San Francisco
ads.body: Jewelry made for your personal journey. Real Crystals, small batch hand crafted, made in San Francisco.  ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ I absolutely LOVE my new necklace. It was a present to my self and I have no regrets! It was such a treat to receive her packaging was almost as exquisite as the piece itself. I'm truly looking forward to gifting or purchasing from Elizabeth again. Blessings - Sarah
drivers: This ad has been running the same picture and words for 76 days, more than triple the age where jewelry ads still pull their weight.
The exact same headline and Sarah review is copied on at least six other live ads, so Meta treats them as one and shows almost none of them.
'Learn more' as the button tells the buyer to read, not buy, so warm shoppers who are ready to check out slip through.

ads.headline: Handcrafted Crystal Jewelry Made in San Francisco
ads.body: Jewelry made for your personal journey. Real Crystals, small batch hand crafted, made in San Francisco.  ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ I absolutely LOVE my new necklace. It was a present to my self and I have no regrets! It was such a treat to receive her packaging was almost as exquisite as the piece itself. I'm truly looking forward to gifting or purchasing from Elizabeth again. Blessings - Sarah
drivers: This is a direct twin of the 'Handcrafted Crystal Jewelry' ad above, and you're paying to make an ad that duplicates one already running.
76 days live means the same people have scrolled past this image over and over, and they stop seeing it.
The homepage link drops shoppers on the front door with no product in view, so they land, look around, and leave.

ads.headline: Handcrafted Crystal Jewelry Made in San Francisco
ads.body: Jewelry made for your personal journey. Real Crystals, small batch hand crafted, made in San Francisco.  ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ I absolutely LOVE my new necklace. It was a present to my self and I have no regrets! It was such a treat to receive her packaging was almost as exquisite as the piece itself. I'm truly looking forward to gifting or purchasing from Elizabeth again. Blessings - Sarah
drivers: Third copy of the same headline and body, live for 76 days, splitting the same small pool of shoppers three ways.
Sarah's review is beautiful, but she never names WHAT she bought, so the ad promises nothing specific.
'Learn more' plus a homepage link sends warm buyers to a maze instead of a checkout, and most give up.

ads.headline: Handcrafted Crystal Jewelry Made in San Francisco
ads.body: Jewelry made for your personal journey. Real Crystals, small batch hand crafted, made in San Francisco.  ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ I absolutely LOVE my new necklace. It was a present to my self and I have no regrets! It was such a treat to receive her packaging was almost as exquisite as the piece itself. I'm truly looking forward to gifting or purchasing from Elizabeth again. Blessings - Sarah
drivers: 77 days old and identical wording to five other live ads, which means Meta has almost certainly stopped serving this one entirely.
There is no urgency, no offer, and no product name in the copy, so a shopper has no reason to click today instead of next month.
You're carrying the ad fee on a piece of content that has aged out of the two-week window jewelry ads live in.

ads.headline: Handcrafted Crystal Jewelry Made in San Francisco
ads.body: Jewelry made for your personal journey. Real Crystals, small batch hand crafted, made in San Francisco.  ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ I absolutely LOVE my new necklace. It was a present to my self and I have no regrets! It was such a treat to receive her packaging was almost as exquisite as the piece itself. I'm truly looking forward to gifting or purchasing from Elizabeth again. Blessings - Sarah
drivers: Fifth live copy of the same 'Handcrafted Crystal Jewelry' ad, so five ads are fighting each other for the same eyeballs.
77 days live means anyone who was ever going to click has clicked, and everyone else has learned to scroll past it.
Landing on the homepage costs you the buyer, because the crystal necklace they came for is nowhere on the page they see.

ads_compact.headline: Stop buying jewelry that says nothing about YOU
ads_compact.body: Why are you settling for less? I get it..life gets busy and somewhere along the way we forget how to feel bold and magnetic. That's why I design jewelry that does't just look good - It Changes How You Feel - the moment you put it on.

ads_compact.headline: Stop Putting Your Jewelry In Plastic Bags
ads_compact.body: Can we talk about the tiny plastic bag situation for a second? You spend good money on beautiful jewelry... then toss it all into a tangled little zip bag before a trip. The result? Knots. Scratches. Broken chains.

hero_concept.hook: Some jewelry you wear. This one you feel.
hero_concept.headline: Wear your intention
hero_concept.primary_text:
The Moon + Crystal Drape Chain Belt is the piece I made for the woman who dresses like she means it.

Hand-set moon charm, real crystal drape, gold chain that moves with you. Not costume, not quiet - an heirloom you actually reach for on a Tuesday.

Small batch, hand made in San Francisco. Shop the new drop while it's here.
hero_concept.visual_direction: Close-up on the belt worn low on the hip over a cream slip dress, warm afternoon window light, San Francisco loft feel.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Right now every ad points at your homepage or repeats the same Sarah review, so no single product ever gets a spotlight. This gives one hero piece its own ad with its own story and its own product page, which is what's been missing.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a soft cream silk slip dress on a hip with the belt draped across the waist, San Francisco artist-loft interior with warm wood floors and a linen curtain behind, with warm late-afternoon golden window light casting a soft directional glow and gentle shadow. Editorial jewelry aesthetic, spiritual and grounded, hand-crafted heirloom feel, close crop that lets the metal and crystals catch the light. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL - preserve the exact warm yellow gold tone of the chain and the moon talisman charm, and preserve the exact natural color, cut, and clarity of the real crystal stones in the drape exactly as shown in the reference - strictly NO silver, white gold, rose gold, brass, or cool metallic tones, NO recoloring or swapping of the crystals, NO synthetic or plastic-looking gemstone finish. STRUCTURE - preserve the exact construction: same number of gold chain strands in the drape, same crystal count and crystal shapes, same moon-shaped talisman charm at its original position, same clasp style, same chain link pattern. Do NOT add or remove crystals, do NOT add or remove chain strands, do NOT change the moon charm shape or swap it for a sun or star, do NOT change the clasp, do NOT alter chain link pattern, do NOT add extra charms or pendants not present in the reference. Render the product's metal, color, finish, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself - text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.
hero_concept.reference_title: Moon + Crystal Drape Chain Belt | Gold Talisman Waist Jewelry

next_step.urgency: Six of your ads are running the exact same 'Handcrafted Crystal Jewelry' copy and will effectively stop earning inside the next week.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Shop Dreamers of Dreams refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Elizabeth, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep - work with us or don't.

method_note: Based only on your public Facebook and Instagram ads: the pictures, the words, and how long each has been running. No access to your account or private numbers.

hero_concepts.hook: Some signs you don't have to go looking for.
hero_concepts.headline: Intuition, worn close
hero_concepts.primary_text:
The Owl of Intuition is for the days you already know the answer and just need something to hold while you act on it.

A 1.5 inch gold owl with a labradorite drop that flashes blue when it catches the light, on an adjustable 20 inch chain so it sits exactly where you want it.

Made by hand in San Francisco. Wrapped in a Dreamers gift bag.
hero_concepts.visual_direction: Close crop on the collarbone with the jawline just in frame, warm late afternoon window light, oatmeal knit top out of focus.
hero_concepts.fills_gap: Your ads show jewelry lying on a table, so a stranger scrolling past has no idea how big any of it actually is. Putting it on a neck answers that in half a second and gives the eye a person to stop on.

hero_concepts.headline: The Owl of Intuition
hero_concepts.primary_text:
The owl doesn't wait for the light. It sees anyway.

That is the whole idea behind this one. A gold owl with a labradorite drop, worn as a reminder that you already have the vision and you are just learning to trust it.

Hand made in small batches in San Francisco.
hero_concepts.visual_direction: The pendant against a deep indigo night sky, single shaft of moonlight, low mist, high contrast bright subject on dark ground.
hero_concepts.fills_gap: Every ad you run right now sells handcrafted crystal jewelry, which is the same thing your whole category says. This one sells what the owl means, and that is a line only you can write.

hero_concepts.hook: It started as a rock in Brazil.
hero_concepts.headline: Wearable art, one of one
hero_concepts.primary_text:
Every Peach Lemurian point is hand selected, then set in a bezel I sculpt and gild myself. No two come out the same, so each one is made once and never again.

Faceted turquoise and gold plated hematite on a 30 inch adjustable chain, with a peach Lemurian point about 3 inches long. I am Earth, I am Water, I am Sky, I am Fire.

Made by Elizabeth Cline in San Francisco.
hero_concepts.visual_direction: Overhead on a linen and weathered wood workbench, raw uncut crystal and worn pliers on the left, finished necklace on the right.
hero_concepts.fills_gap: Nothing in your ads right now shows that a person makes these by hand, so the price has to defend itself. Putting the raw stone next to the finished piece turns wearable art into something they can see.
